stages.name: HS-JEPA Core Architecture
stages.role: Defines the reusable human-understanding mechanism before any sleep-competition target names are introduced.
stages.boundary: The core must not depend on S2, public LB sensors, submission files, or manual row ids.

stages.name: HS-JEPA Core Reference Run
stages.role: Executes the dataset-free core on synthetic context/listener/action inputs to prove the architecture is not only a report.
stages.boundary: This stage is architecture-only; it must not read competition data or sensor observations.

stages.name: HS-JEPA Core Module Benchmark
stages.role: Tests the reusable core across generic human-state worlds and compares full core against module-removal policies.
stages.boundary: This stage is core-only; it proves architecture behavior without sleep labels or public sensors.

stages.name: OG Raw Lifestyle Context
stages.role: Provides train labels, submission key contract, and raw lifelog items.
outputs: raw/context feature artifacts used by upstream HS-JEPA modules
stages.boundary: This stage is competition data, not external/private data.

stages.name: Public LB Sensor Ledger
stages.role: Uses public submission observations as a sensor for hidden row-target action response.
stages.boundary: This is not an OG-only claim; it is the competition-specific sensor path.

stages.name: Human-State Listener Context
stages.role: Turns lifestyle/cohort context into target/cell orientation diagnostics.
stages.boundary: Human-state is an orientation diagnostic, not a standalone row selector.

stages.name: OG-only Assignment Teacher Probe
stages.role: Tests whether human-state geometry can replace the public-sensor row-target assignment teacher.
stages.boundary: The probe currently measures the gap; it does not prove pure OG-only deployment.

stages.name: Assignment Gap Decomposition
stages.role: Decomposes public-sensitive row-target assignment into target-route information and hidden row-support information.
stages.boundary: This is a bottleneck decomposition, not a deployable row-support sensor.

stages.name: Hidden Row-Support Sensor Transfer Probe
stages.role: Learns row-support from one public-sensitive teacher world and tests transfer to another teacher world.
evidence: Mean cell recall with stage prior: 0.3289
stages.boundary: This is transfer evidence for a row-support sensor, not yet an action-grade deployment decoder.

stages.name: Masked Row-Support Objective Probe
stages.role: Tests whether hidden row-support can serve as a JEPA target representation predicted from masked human/context views.
stages.boundary: This supports HS-JEPA representation learning, but weak group-heldout stress blocks direct deployment as an action decoder.

stages.name: Row-Support Strict Action Decoder
stages.role: Translates masked row-support into route-conserving, toxicity-filtered row-target action candidates.
stages.boundary: This is an LB-informative big bet with a route-gain tradeoff, not a safe default submission.

stages.name: Route-Frontier Action Decoder
stages.role: Selects route-manifold frontier actions before trusting row-support and toxicity gates.
stages.boundary: This tests whether action-grade decoding should start from route-frontier selection rather than support-first selection.

stages.name: Route-Toxicity Fusion Decoder
stages.role: Composes route-frontier action ordering with factorized broad-public and hard-world action-health gating.
evidence: Variant scores: [{'variant': 's2_route_toxicity_fusion', 'changed_cells': 8, 'broad_route_z': -0.06361725497399186, 'toxicity_matched_safety_z': 0.0, 'toxicity_matched_fusion_z': 0.00022199529973856787, 'upload_safe': True}, {'variant': 'seed_route_toxicity_fusion', 'changed_cells': 8, 'broad_route_z': -0.05413537720642773, 'toxicity_matched_safety_z': 0.0, 'toxicity_matched_fusion_z': 0.00022199529973856787, 'upload_safe': True}, {'variant': 'open_route_toxicity_fusion', 'changed_cells': 4, 'broad_route_z': -0.16743111973717828, 'toxicity_matched_safety_z': 0.00022199529973856787, 'toxicity_matched_fusion_z': 0.0, 'upload_safe': True}, {'variant': 's2_driver_safe_route_fusion', 'changed_cells': 20, 'broad_route_z': 2.5212391425980725, 'toxicity_matched_safety_z': 1.4350151378530516, 'toxicity_matched_fusion_z': 3.333896510179827, 'upload_safe': True}, {'variant': 'seed_driver_safe_route_fusion', 'changed_cells': 20, 'broad_route_z': 1.956452255410393, 'toxicity_matched_safety_z': 1.1375544203021746, 'toxicity_matched_fusion_z': 4.040831045742473, 'upload_safe': True}, {'variant': 'open_driver_safe_route_fusion', 'changed_cells': 20, 'broad_route_z': 1.2492144363720237, 'toxicity_matched_safety_z': 1.1862432357203119, 'toxicity_matched_fusion_z': 1.8706591048812475, 'upload_safe': True}]
stages.boundary: This is a competition-adapter action solver; it does not prove private-LB safety or pure OG-only assignment.

stages.name: Decoder-Order Jury Solver
stages.role: Releases only row-target actions independently selected by route-frontier and route-toxicity fusion decoders.
stages.boundary: This tests whether HS-JEPA action decoding should be a cross-decoder listener jury rather than a single route or toxicity score.

stages.name: Decoder Boundary Tomography Solver
stages.role: Splits cells rejected by strict cross-decoder jury into weak-consensus, route-only, and fusion-only action worlds.
stages.boundary: This is the too-conservative-jury diagnostic; consensus-shadow cells are not safe until public LB observes them.

stages.name: Core-Mediated Action Release
stages.role: Converts real sleep-adapter row-target actions into the generic HS-JEPA core API before release.
stages.boundary: This proves the core API can drive adapter actions mechanically; public LB still decides whether that core release equation is action-grade.

stages.name: Core Release Ablation Probe
stages.role: Removes listener responsibility, action-health, or invariant energy from the real adapter release equation to make HS-JEPA core constraints falsifiable.
stages.boundary: This is an architecture falsification probe; module-removal public LB is needed before claiming a removed module improves the adapter.

stages.name: Core-Health Calibrated Release
stages.role: Uses dataset-free action-health false-positive lift as a release prior for real sleep-adapter row-target actions.
stages.boundary: This is the direct bridge from generic core behavior to adapter release; public LB must still decide whether the guard is too conservative.

stages.name: Cross-Listener Transport Decoder
stages.role: Uses target-listener posterior as a transport calibrator over route/fusion/core-safe action cells instead of directly generating actions.

Negative sensor: {'file': 'submission_hsjepa_target_listener_route_lift_s2hub_listener_lift_jackpot_f2ab2816_uploadsafe.csv', 'public_lb': 0.5680255019, 'interpretation': 'direct listener-lift is not action-grade; use listener posterior only as a release/calibration prior'}
stages.boundary: This stage does not claim listener posterior is a direct action head; it tests whether it can safely calibrate already supported actions.

stages.name: Counterfactual Listener-Dropout Solver
stages.role: Treats each listener as a removable view and releases only row-target action fields that remain coherent under listener dropout, while using failed public sensors as toxicity evidence.
evidence: Claim: HS-JEPA should release row-target actions that survive counterfactual listener dropout and avoid directions marked toxic by previous public sensors.
stages.boundary: This is a high-information public/private equation sensor; it can validate listener-invariant action health or kill that release geometry.

stages.name: Spectral Public-Tangent Solver
stages.role: Treats post-H057 public failures as a low-rank negative representation and tests anti-tangent versus orthogonal residual action release.
stages.boundary: This stage proves a negative representation geometry, not that the inverse direction is label-valid before public LB observes it.

stages.name: Negative Tangent Invariant Projection Solver
stages.role: Projects the low-rank public-bad negative representation onto train target-route and subject-prior invariant coordinates before releasing row-target actions.
evidence: Core claim: HS-JEPA representations become action-grade only after projection onto invariant human-state routes.
stages.boundary: This stage tests whether a negative representation is action-grade only after invariant projection; public LB must still validate the generated action field.

stages.name: LB-Conditioned Responsibility Solver
stages.role: Treats public LB as an external scalar listener and estimates row-target action responsibility from observed submission loss deltas.
stages.boundary: This is a scalar-listener inversion sensor; if public LB rejects it, responsibility is diagnostic but not yet a portable action equation.

stages.name: Mixture-Listener Responsibility Solver
stages.role: Factors scalar public response into latent listener heads and tests consensus, conflict, and Q/S target-specific routing as HS-JEPA action decoders.
stages.boundary: This is the next thesis-level sensor: if public LB accepts target-split routing, HS-JEPA moves from scalar listener responsibility to latent listener mixture routing.

stages.name: Public/Private Subset Tomography Solver
stages.role: Decomposes scalar public feedback into public subset inclusion, hidden label direction, private safety, and action toxicity before releasing row-target actions.
stages.boundary: This is a public/private listener-equation sensor; public LB must decide whether the inferred subset/label decomposition is action-grade.

stages.name: Anti-Listener Toxicity Equation Solver
stages.role: Uses failed listener-derived public actions as negative teachers and releases only inverse or vetoed row-target actions that survive private-safety and route/action-health constraints.
stages.boundary: This is a negative-listener action-equation sensor; until public LB confirms it, listener failures are diagnostic evidence rather than a proven action generator.

stages.name: Action Decoder Ablation Suite
stages.role: Ranks toxicity-first, support-first, route-first, route-toxicity fusion, decoder-jury, boundary-tomography, core-mediated, core-release-ablation, core-health-calibrated, cross-listener transport, listener-dropout, spectral, invariant-projection, LB-conditioned responsibility, mixture-listener, public/private subset-tomography, and anti-listener toxicity alternatives as HS-JEPA module ablations.
stages.boundary: This prioritizes public-sensor experiments; it is not a calibrated LB predictor.

stages.name: Q/S Route Energy Model
stages.role: Learns a target-route manifold from train labels and scores whether an action breaks it.
stages.boundary: Route energy proves candidate-pool structure, not private leaderboard safety.

stages.name: Listener-Invariant Contrastive Probe
stages.role: Tests whether listener responsibility and route-invariant action health select the same bundles.
stages.boundary: This stage is a diagnostic; it does not create a new submission.

stages.name: Private-Safe Toxicity Probe
stages.role: Tests whether toxicity head generalizes across bad public anchors and selects safer cells than matched nulls.
stages.boundary: This stage supports toxicity diagnostics, not a private-LB safety guarantee.

stages.name: Hard-World Toxicity Factorization Probe
stages.role: Tests whether the H088 hard-world toxicity mode is anti-correlated with broad public-bad toxicity.
stages.boundary: This diagnostic stage says action-health should be factorized; the following decoder stage is the submission translation.

stages.name: Factorized Toxicity Decoder Candidate
stages.role: Translates broad-public and hard-world toxicity heads into upload-safe row-target action candidates.
stages.boundary: This creates competition submissions, but their public/private score impact remains unverified until external submission.

stages.name: Factorized Toxicity Decoder Stress Audit
stages.role: Compares factorized decoder variants against target-only and source-matched feasible null action fields.
stages.boundary: This is local action-health evidence; external public/private LB remains a separate sensor.

stages.name: Public-Sensitive Driver Action Field
stages.role: Selects sparse row-target cells that public sensor evidence says are worth moving.
stages.boundary: This stage is deliberately separated from the OG human-state representation claim.

stages.name: Route-Conserving S2 Bridge Decoder
stages.role: Pairs driver cells with same-row bridge cells that lower route energy and repeatedly use S2 as listener/hub.
evidence: Primary route delta vs null: -0.02457 vs -0.01090
stages.boundary: S2 is a decoder listener/hub in this action space, not a universal sleep physiology claim.

stages.name: Role-Based Submission Packager
stages.role: Packages three role-based outputs without requiring historical version names.
stages.boundary: Upload safety is a format guarantee, not a score guarantee.

stages.name: Mechanism Ablation Knockout
stages.role: Records which alternative worldviews public sensors and local stress audits killed or preserved.
stages.boundary: This explains mechanism evidence; it is not a new private-score guarantee.

stages.name: General Architecture Boundary
stages.role: Separates reusable HS-JEPA modules from the sleep-competition S2/public-sensor instantiation.
stages.boundary: The current strongest case study still uses a public-sensor assignment teacher.

stages.name: Sleep Competition Adapter
stages.role: Maps HS-JEPA Core into Q/S listeners, route energy, public-sensor action evidence, and upload-safe sparse row-target outputs.
stages.boundary: This adapter is a competition case study; it is not the general HS-JEPA architecture.

stages.name: Core/Adapter Boundary Audit
stages.role: Statically verifies that HS-JEPA Core has no operational dependency on competition adapters and that the adapter depends on the core contract.
stages.boundary: This verifies architecture separation; it does not create a new prediction or score guarantee.

stages.name: Claim Readiness and Paper Packet
stages.role: Converts the runnable package into paper/team-facing evidence and method text.
evidence: Method title: Human-State JEPA: General Architecture with a Route-Conserving S2 Bridge Case Study
stages.boundary: Paper claims must keep representation, public sensor, and action decoder separated.

boundary.human_state_role: orientation diagnostic, not complete row-target assignment solver
boundary.competition_decoder_role: public-sensitive row-target action solver with route-conserving S2 bridge

config.name: listener_confirmed_shadow
config.probe_role: tests whether target-listener confirmed shadow cells are safer than broad shadow release

config.name: objective_listener_island_probe
config.probe_role: tests whether an objective-listener island exists outside the current public row-state support

cross_listener_transport_negative_sensor.interpretation: direct listener-lift is not action-grade; use listener posterior only as a release/calibration prior

counterfactual_listener_dropout_top_vari.worldview: Negative public sensors may have failed because they mixed good high-health cells with toxic extras; therefore release a larger listener-dropout field with only mild toxicity damping.

config.name: dropout_fullfield_aggressive
config.worldview: Negative public sensors may have failed because they mixed good high-health cells with toxic extras; therefore release a larger listener-dropout field with only mild toxicity damping.

counterfactual_listener_dropout_top_vari.worldview: If listener-dropout healthy cells repeatedly align with bad public sensors, the hidden action may be on the wrong side of the public/private equation; test a small opposite-direction correction.

config.name: toxic_direction_inversion
config.worldview: If listener-dropout healthy cells repeatedly align with bad public sensors, the hidden action may be on the wrong side of the public/private equation; test a small opposite-direction correction.
